title: A new Ising/tricritical-Ising interface: From ${W}_3$ symmetry to Rydberg atoms
abstract: We consider interfaces between critical spin-chains in different universality classes, described in the continuum limit by defect/interface conformal field theory (DCFT/ICFT). We find a new conformal interface between the Tricritical Ising (TIM) and the Ising CFT. We also explore the possibility of its experimental realizations in the context of Rydberg atom arrays. Our analysis emphasizes non-invertible symmetries and consistency under modular transformations, and uses defect couplings and the defect spectrum -- including in the case of mixed boundary conditions -- to make sharp experimental predictions. The structure of the observables hinges on a newly discovered pattern of emergent ${W}_3$ chiral symmetry for the Tricritical-Ising/Ising interface.
summary: This paper identifies a new type of conformal interface between two different universality classes, the Ising and Tricritical Ising models. It reveals that this interface possesses an emergent W3 chiral symmetry and proposes that these theoretical predictions can be tested using Rydberg atom arrays.
main_problem: The discovery and characterization of a new conformal interface between the Ising and Tricritical Ising universality classes, and the investigation of its emergent W3 chiral symmetry.
main_result: The authors identify a new conformal interface that exhibits an emergent W3 chiral symmetry and demonstrate that this interface can be experimentally realized using Rydberg atom arrays.
method: The study combines theoretical Conformal Field Theory (CFT) analysis, including modular bootstrap and symmetry gauging, with numerical simulations using Matrix Product States (MPS) and DMRG.
model_or_system: The system consists of interfaces between critical spin-chains, specifically the Ising and Tricritical Ising models, with a potential experimental implementation in Rydberg atom arrays in a ladder geometry.
key_observables: Energy spectrum of the Hamiltonian, scaling dimensions of operators, partition functions (closed and open string channels), and boundary state amplitudes.
important_parameters: Interface coupling strength (hb), critical coupling ratios (K*/J*), and Rydberg atom parameters such as detuning (delta) and Rabi frequency (Omega).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the W3 symmetry and uses the k=1 specialization for the Ising/TIM interface; it also assumes the accuracy of specific modular S-transformation matrices from existing literature.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the conformal interface; Figure 4 shows the open-string sector spectrum for mixed boundary conditions; Figure 5 shows the spectrum for the W3 invariant state; Figure 7 compares the interface spectrum with the KW-twisted sector.
paper_structure: The paper begins by introducing the new Ising/TIM interface, moves to the theoretical derivation of the W3 symmetry and boundary state construction, presents numerical validation via MPS/DMRG, and concludes with the proposed experimental realization in Rydberg arrays.
why_interesting: It provides a concrete link between high-level algebraic structures (W3 symmetry) and controllable experimental platforms (Rydberg atoms), offering a way to study non-invertible symmetries and conformal interfaces in many-body systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: A conformal interface on the plane becomes defects separating two different quantum critical phases on a circle, after a conformal transformation.
FIG. 2: The phase transition on the Ising/TIM interface. The plot is symmetric with respect to hb = 0.

FIG. 3: The states of Ising/TIM interface at critical coupling, which corresponds to the open-string channel spectrum of the folded theory. The small horizontal lines are the theoretical prediction, and the dots are the numerical data. The blue and red dots correspond to the Z2 even and odd sectors, respectively. The system size is NIsing + NTIM = 256.

FIG. 4: The open-string sector spectrum of mixed boundary conditions. We apply the FM boundary condition on one side and the factorized boundary condition C1 on the other side. The system size is NIsing + NTIM = 256. The blue and red dots correspond to the Z2 even and odd sectors, respectively.

FIG. 5: The open-string sector spectrum of mixed boundary conditions. We take the FM boundary condition on one side and the AFM boundary on the other side. The system size is NIsing + NTIM = 256. The blue and red dots correspond to the Z2 even and odd sectors, respectively.
